title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
description: If the system doesn't properly validate and sanitize user input, attackers could inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, XSS) into data fields, leading to data corruption, unauthorized access, or system compromise.
attack_vector: Malicious input provided through forms, APIs, or other user interfaces that bypasses input validation.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization techniques.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
